title: ADLC Pack Manifest
description: The vendor-neutral format contract for an ADLC pack manifest, version 1.0. Validates a CONFORMANT pack (spec.md section 5). An experimental pack MAY omit 'evals' but is then not conformant. Certification programs extend this with their own fields; this schema is the base they extend.

name.description: Kebab-case pack name (1-64 chars, lowercase, digits, hyphens; no leading/trailing hyphen).

version.description: A version string. SHOULD follow Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 (spec 5.1). SemVer is checked advisorily by conformance check P6, not hard-enforced here, so the SHOULD stays overridable with a stated reason. The official SemVer spec and regex are at https://semver.org.

description.description: One line. What the pack does and when it applies.

license.description: SPDX license identifier or SPDX LicenseRef. OpenADLC packs use LicenseRef-OpenADLC-Source-Available-1.0; standard content uses CC-BY-4.0.

author.description: Optional. The pack's author. Kept for harness compatibility (a Claude Code plugin manifest carries it).

owner.description: Optional in the schema; a conformant pack declares one (Law L3, currency needs an owner). Who maintains the pack and how to reach them.

adlc.description: The ADLC spec version this pack targets.

units.description: Per-kind unit counts (spec.md section 5.2): how many units the pack ships of each kind. A rule is carried under 'references'. Conformance check P2 requires at least one nonzero count.

evals.description: The eval bar the pack clears (spec.md section 5.3, Law L5): 'conformance' is the structural eval; 'conformance+gate' also ships a behavioral eval. Required for a conformant pack.

capabilities.description: Default-deny capability declaration (spec.md section 5.4). Omitted keys mean the pack does not take that capability. Declared capabilities MUST match actual behavior.

exec.description: Scoped commands the pack runs (e.g. 'npm run lint', 'npx playwright test'). Breadth (a general shell, an eval flag) is declared via 'exec-broad', not hidden in this scoped list.

exec-broad.description: True if the pack runs a general shell or arbitrary commands (bash, sh, eval, 'python -c', 'node -e', etc.). Expressible but high-risk: tier policy gates it (banned in open tiers, review-with-justification in certified). A pack that can run anything MUST set this true; it MUST NOT be hidden as a scoped 'exec' entry.

exec-installer.description: True if the pack runs dependency installers that execute arbitrary upstream code (npm install, pip install). The dangerous half of exec; declared separately so reviewers can route it.

network.description: Scoped allowlist of named hosts (optionally :port). Wildcard or broad access is declared via 'network-broad', not here. Port-range (0-65535) is not validated at the format layer.

network-broad.description: True if the pack reaches arbitrary or wildcard hosts. Expressible but high-risk: tier policy gates it (banned in open tiers, review-with-justification in certified).

fs-read.description: Path scopes the pack reads. Default is the workspace.

fs-write.description: Path scopes the pack writes. Default is none.

env.description: Environment variables or secret names the pack reads. Default is none.

subprocess.description: True if the pack spawns long-running processes or daemons.
